title: Today's AI Brief: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation
summary: A look at the Vatican's unexpected alliance with Anthropic, the challenges of agentic AI adoption, and the chaos caused by AI agents.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation
imageCaption: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation
imageSourceTitle: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation

body.text: I'm intrigued by the Vatican's partnership with Anthropic, a leading AI research organization. Pope Leo XIV recently presented his first encyclical on artificial intelligence and invited Christopher Olah, co-founder of Anthropic, to speak. This unexpected alliance between the Catholic Church and Silicon Valley has raised eyebrows. According to Wired, the encyclical presentation signals a new era of cooperation between two seemingly disparate entities. I wonder what sparked this collaboration and what it might mean for the future of AI development.

A report from MIT Technology Review highlights the challenges of adopting agentic AI in enterprises. While 85% of organizations aim to be agentic within three years, 76% say their current operations and infrastructure can't support that change. It's like trying to retrofit an old car with a new engine – it might look good on paper, but it's not quite that simple. The disconnect between ambition and execution is a significant hurdle for companies looking to harness the power of AI agents. I'm curious to see how organizations will overcome these challenges and successfully integrate AI into their operations.
The Chaos Caused by AI Agents

AI agents have plunged the tech world into chaos, and Wired has a great story on how this happened. It all started with coding tools like Anthropic's Claude, which have become so popular that they've created a community of 'Claudeholics.' These techies are swept up by the possibilities of AI-powered coding and are now networking with like-minded individuals. I've heard that some developers are even attending meetups and sharing their experiences with AI agents. It's a wild ride, and I'm not sure if it's a bubble waiting to burst or a revolution just getting started.

I've also been reading about the growing use of AI agents in various industries. For instance, some companies are using AI-powered chatbots to improve customer service, while others are leveraging AI-driven tools to enhance their marketing efforts. It's clear that AI agents are having a significant impact on the tech world, and it will be interesting to see how they continue to shape the future of AI development.

As I look at these headlines, I'm struck by the rapid pace of change in the AI landscape. The Vatican's partnership with Anthropic might seem unusual, but it's a reminder that AI is a force that transcends traditional boundaries. From the challenges of agentic AI adoption to the chaos caused by AI agents, it's clear that we're in a period of significant upheaval. My read is that we're just beginning to scratch the surface of what's possible with AI, and it's going to be a bumpy ride – but also an exciting one.
